title: How do you find editors for your Faceless YouTube Channel who are actually good?

transcript.text: Can you explain how to find skilled editors who understand the niche so they use the correct B rolls? This part I struggle with a lot. >> So if your [music] content is truly unique and branded, you can't just hire experience because nobody has experience in a thing [music] that didn't exist until you made it. This is why I love the concept of cloning by Mr. Beast, [music] which is basically you do stuff and let them watch. You then do it together with them so [music] that they can ask you questions in a process and you review the results. Then you let them do it fully but you still check the result and then the last [music] step is you delegate it entirely trusting them with the process. I think even if they did [music] not have the original skill when you hired them.
